rationale: The cited 2005 paper is a direct theory/mechanism fit: it shows extrinsic marketing cues (price) change efficacy outcomes via expectancies, while the citing 2025 paper studies another extrinsic cue (legality) changing perceived efficacy through an inference mechanism (accessibility/strength). Given this strong construct overlap in efficacy-belief formation and the confirmed DOI citation match, this is not an obvious incidental mention and merits full relation review.
